name: Interpretation Forecast review for Senomorphics bundle
summary: The staged interpretation is calibrated. It adds PMID 42251040 as a preclinical mianserin/Ca2+/SASP signal with animal lifespan and human-derived cell support, while keeping Senomorphics at human_biomarker_signal, momentum steady, and confidence low. The outlook continues to require treated-human trials linking senescence/SASP biomarker movement to sustained function, tissue resilience, disease outcomes, and acceptable safety before any stage or LEV-relevant forecast upgrade. The two registry refreshes are handled as factual study metadata, not evidence upgrades.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged outlook adds mianserin to strongest current evidence as a human-cell and mouse healthspan/lifespan signal but keeps the public evidence stage at human_biomarker_signal.
findings.why_it_matters: Animal lifespan and human-derived cell senescence data are important for plausibility, but they do not establish treated-human function, disease modification, or prevention benefit.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; publish as written if editorial approves, preserving the no-treated-human and preclinical limitations.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged outlook adds the new finding and source to supporting_finding_ids, supporting_source_ids, strongest_current_evidence, and a dedicated supporting_evidence entry with limitations.
findings.why_it_matters: A track-outlook change should not rely on a loose source citation; the public rationale needs concrete source-to-study-to-finding mapping.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; the evidence map is explicit enough for the proposed unchanged-stage outlook refresh.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged registry updates clarify ClinicalTrials.gov status and completion horizons but do not alter the human biomarker evidence basis or outlook rating.
findings.why_it_matters: No-results registry status, even with an active parent metformin registry, should not be treated as new efficacy or safety evidence.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep registry metadata out of stage, momentum, and confidence changes.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged outlook adds calcium-homeostasis, serotonin-receptor, and central-nervous-system tradeoffs to the existing immune, wound-healing, cancer, metabolic, and pleiotropy cautions.
findings.why_it_matters: Mianserin should not be presented as a simple SASP-modulating aging intervention without acknowledging pathway attribution and preventive-use safety uncertainty.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; retain the staged safety and attribution caveats through publication.
